study_label: Contractor and Reyes (2026), Session one test
treatment: AI allowed while learning a new topic

study_label: Contractor and Reyes (2026), Session two test
outcome: Test score, Session 2 (1 week later, no AI)
treatment: AI allowed while learning a new topic

study_label: Contractor and Reyes (2026), Session one essay
outcome: Essay quality, Session 1 (written with AI access)
treatment: AI allowed while learning a new topic

study_label: Contractor and Reyes (2026), Session two essay
outcome: Essay quality, Session 2 (1 week later, no AI)
treatment: AI allowed while learning a new topic

study_label: Barcaui, Knowledge retention test (20-item MCQ, 4
outcome: Knowledge retention test (20-item MCQ, 45 days post-intervention)
notes: Negative = AI group learned less. 85 of 120 completed retention test. AI group spent 45% less time studying.

study_label: Bastani et al., Normalized grade on assisted practice pr
outcome: Normalized grade on assisted practice problems (0-1 scale)
control: No AI access (textbooks and notes only)
notes: Classroom-level randomization. Regression with session/grader/grade/teacher FEs. Clustered SE at classroom level.

study_label: Bastani et al., Normalized grade on assisted practice pr
outcome: Normalized grade on assisted practice problems (0-1 scale)
treatment: GPT Tutor (GPT-4 with guardrails: Socratic hints, teacher-designed prompts)
control: No AI access (textbooks and notes only)
notes: Prompt includes correct solutions, common mistakes, instructions not to give away answers.

study_label: Bastani et al., GPT Base
outcome: Normalized grade on unassisted exam (0-1 scale)
control: No AI access (textbooks and notes only)
notes: KEY: Unrestricted GPT-4 during practice => 17% worse on unassisted exam. Measures learning transfer without AI.

study_label: Bastani et al., GPT Tutor
outcome: Normalized grade on unassisted exam (0-1 scale)
control: No AI access (textbooks and notes only)
notes: Guardrails mitigate negative learning effect. Point estimate near zero, not significant.

study_label: De Simone et al., Total score (weighted) on final assessme
notes: ITT. 6-week intervention, 12 sessions of 90 min. School FE and baseline controls.

study_label: De Simone et al., English
notes: Main outcome. Equivalent to 1.5 years of business-as-usual schooling.

study_label: De Simone et al., retention
notes: Broader curriculum than intervention content. Spillovers beyond intervention-specific content.

study_label: Fan et al., Essay score improvement (post-revision m
notes: Four-arm RCT. AI restricted to task-relevant conversations. ESL writing task. Tukey HSD adjusted.

study_label: Fan et al., Knowledge gain (pre-post test on AI in e
outcome: Knowledge gain (pre-post test on AI in education)
notes: Post-test within one day. Not significant.

study_label: Hausman et al., Grade in advanced AI-compatible courses
treatment: Students who began in 2022-23 (ChatGPT available) with AI-compatible intro courses
control: Students who began in 2021-22 (no ChatGPT) with AI-compatible intro courses
notes: Effect of early AI exposure on subsequent advanced course performance. Table 5 col. 3.

study_label: Hausman et al., Course grade (0-100 scale), Year 2
notes: Year 2 after rollout; effect larger than Year 1. ~80% AI adoption in 2023-24.

study_label: Hausman et al., Course grade (0-100 scale), Year 1
treatment: AI-compatible courses (<=60% grade from AI-incompatible work) after ChatGPT rollout (2022-23)
control: AI-incompatible courses (>=90% grade from in-class exams/lab work)
notes: ITT effect (AI availability, not use). Student FE + semester FE + course controls. Grades avg ~86. ~30% AI adoption in 2022-23.

study_label: Hausman et al., 25th percentile grade
notes: Course-semester level. Largest effects for weakest students. +3 points at 25th percentile.

study_label: Hausman et al., Course grade (0-100)
notes: Baseline DID. ITT effect of AI availability. Student + semester FE, clustered by course.

study_label: Henkel et al., Math growth score (endline - baseline, 3
treatment: Rori AI math tutor (2x30min/week for ~6 months)
notes: School-level randomization (5 treatment, 6 control). d=0.36 Morris pooled SD. Cost ~$5/student.

study_label: Kalam et al., Initial quiz score (10-item MCQ, with re
outcome: Initial quiz score (10-item MCQ, with resources)
notes: Quiz WITH resources. Very small sample. Scores: 9.60 vs 6.64.
quality_flags: small N (33); no SE/CI; non-standard outcome

study_label: Kalam et al., Retention quiz (10-item MCQ, no resource
outcome: Retention quiz (10-item MCQ, no resources, 1 week later)
notes: Not significant (ANOVA p=0.118). Underpowered (43% power). Scores: 6.20 vs 4.36.
quality_flags: small N (33); no SE/CI; non-standard outcome

study_label: Kazemitabaar et al., Authoring task correctness (training pha
outcome: Authoring task correctness (training phase, with AI)
treatment: Codex AI code generator access during training
notes: During training WITH AI access. t(67)=6.92.
quality_flags: non-standard outcome; SE back-calculated from N and Cohen's d

study_label: Kazemitabaar et al., post-test
outcome: Authoring task correctness (immediate post-test, no AI)
treatment: Codex AI code generator access during training
notes: Post-test 1 day after training; no AI access. Not significant.
quality_flags: SE back-calculated from N and Cohen's d

study_label: Kazemitabaar et al., retention
outcome: Modifying task correctness (1-week retention, no AI)
treatment: Codex AI code generator access during training
notes: Retention test 1 week after training; no AI. Marginally significant.
quality_flags: SE back-calculated from N and Cohen's d

study_label: Kestin et al., Post-test score (physics content mastery
treatment: AI tutor (GPT-4 with pedagogical prompts and scaffolding)
control: In-class active learning (peer instruction, group work)
notes: Crossover design. OLS effect size 0.63 SD is underestimate due to ceiling effect; quantile regression gives 0.73-1.3 SD. AI group median time 49 min vs 60 min in-class. Mann-Whitney z=-5.6.

study_label: Kestin et al., Post-test score (quantile regression, av
outcome: Post-test score (quantile regression, avoids ceiling effect)
treatment: AI tutor (GPT-4 with pedagogical prompts and scaffolding)
control: In-class active learning (peer instruction, group work)
notes: Preferred estimate: quantile regression range 0.73-1.3 SD avoids ceiling effect in post-test. Midpoint ~1.0 SD.

study_label: Kim et al., Problems solved per day (log)
treatment: Post-solution AI tutor (GenAI activated after answer submission)
control: Baseline digital practice (correctness feedback + static solution)
notes: DiD exploiting staggered rollout. Student and date FEs. 2.1M student-day obs.
quality_flags: not RCT; no SD effect; non-standard outcome; active control

study_label: Kim et al., Average daily correctness rate
treatment: Post-solution AI tutor (GenAI activated after answer submission)
notes: DiD design. SD estimated from IQR, d approx. Low-performing students benefit most.

study_label: Kreijkes et al., Literal retention (cued recall + MC)
notes: Within-subject (Group 1). Test 3 days post-learning. Negative = LLM worse than notes.

study_label: Kreijkes et al., Comprehension (bridging/knowledge-based
notes: Within-subject (Group 1). Test 3 days post-learning.

study_label: Kumar et al., Test-phase accuracy (SAT-style math, MCQ
treatment: Try First + Customized GPT-4 explanation (pre-prompted with problem-solving strategies)
notes: z=-4.20. Slightly larger effect than stock LLM. MTurk workers, not students.

study_label: Kumar et al., Test-phase accuracy (SAT-style math, MCQ
treatment: Try First + Stock GPT-4 explanation with correct answer
notes: z=-3.46. Accuracy from figure (approximate). 2 test questions per participant. MTurk workers, not students.

study_label: LearnLM Team, Knowledge transfer (correct on first que
outcome: Knowledge transfer (correct on first question of next topic)
notes: Key learning outcome: transfer to new topic. OR=1.6 [1.2, 2.0]. P(LearnLM>hint)>99.9%.

study_label: LearnLM Team, Mistake remediation (correct on 2nd atte
notes: Bayesian logistic regression. OR=7.4 [5.1, 11.0]. 165 students. Human tutor supervised LearnLM messages.
quality_flags: no SD effect; non-standard outcome; active control

study_label: LearnLM Team, Knowledge transfer (correct on first que
outcome: Knowledge transfer (correct on first question of next topic)
notes: OR=1.3 [0.9, 1.7]. P(LearnLM>human)=93.6%. Headline: +5.5pp over human tutoring on transfer.

study_label: Lehmann et al., Grade on subsequent coding questions (0-
outcome: Grade on subsequent coding questions (0-1 scale)
treatment: Higher cumulative ChatGPT use (measured by text similarity)
notes: Study 1 (field). Continuous treatment. Negative = more past ChatGPT use harms future performance. 6,594 student-question obs.
quality_flags: not RCT; no SD effect; non-standard outcome

study_label: Lehmann et al., Study 3
treatment: Access to unrestricted ChatGPT (GPT-3.5) during learning phase; copy-paste enabled
notes: Study 3 (replication with copy-paste). d~0.42 back-calculated. Marginally significant in t-test, null in regression.

study_label: Lehmann et al., Topic understanding (post-test | learnin
notes: Exploratory. Treated subjects only. Copy-paste as exogenous shifter of substitutive use.

study_label: Lehmann et al., Grade on subsequent coding questions (0-
outcome: Grade on subsequent coding questions (0-1 scale, IV)
treatment: Higher cumulative ChatGPT code similarity (substitutive use)
notes: FE2SLS using ChatGPT service outages as IV. N=6,594 obs. Continuous treatment.
quality_flags: not RCT; no SD effect; non-standard outcome

study_label: Lehmann et al., Study 2
treatment: Access to unrestricted ChatGPT (GPT-3.5) during learning phase; no copy-paste
notes: Study 2. No copy-paste available (unintended). d~0.25 back-calculated from means/SDs. Not significant.

study_label: Lira et al., retention
notes: Study 2 follow-up. 87% retention. Effect persists at 1 day.

study_label: Lira et al., Writing quality (test phase, no AI)
notes: Study 4. AI beats 49 professional editors (avg 25 yrs experience).

study_label: Lira et al., Study 5
notes: Study 5. Example-only as effective as practice with AI (d=0.03 difference). Mechanism: learning by example.

study_label: Lira et al., Study 2
outcome: Writing quality (GPT-4o rated, test phase, no AI)
notes: Study 2. Test phase: writing without AI. Pre-registered.

study_label: Nie et al., Exam participation rate
treatment: Access to GPT-4 chat interface (advertisement + access)
notes: ITT. Only 14.2% of treatment group used GPT-4. Bonferroni-corrected p=0.020.

study_label: Nie et al., Exam score (LATE, imputed for missingnes
notes: LATE for adopters (14.2% compliance), imputing for missingness. 90% CI. Preferred estimate. Not significant after Bonferroni.

study_label: Vanzo et al., Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), po
outcome: Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), pooled
control: Standard homework submitted via online platform
notes: Not significant. Pooled across 3rd and 5th year. One-sided t-test. Preprint.

study_label: Vanzo et al., Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), 3r
outcome: Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), 3rd year only
notes: Marginally significant (one-sided). Objective/grammar homework type.

study_label: Vanzo et al., Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), 5t
outcome: Learning gain on MCQ test (24 items), 5th year only
notes: Essentially zero effect. Mismatch between essay homework and objective test.

study_label: Wang et al., Exit ticket passed (binary) - low-rated
outcome: Exit ticket passed (binary) - low-rated tutor subgroup
treatment: Tutor CoPilot (AI suggestions to low-rated tutors)
notes: Bottom tercile of tutor quality. Low-rated tutors + CoPilot = high-rated tutors without it.
quality_flags: N unknown; no SE/CI; non-standard outcome
estimation_method: OLS (FE, clustered; treatment x tutor-rating interaction)

study_label: Wang et al., Exit ticket passed (binary)
treatment: Tutor CoPilot (AI provides real-time suggestions to human tutors)
control: Business-as-usual tutoring (no AI assistance for tutors)
notes: AI assists the TUTOR, not student directly. Tutor-level randomization (782 tutors). 4,136 sessions. $20/tutor/year.
estimation_method: OLS (school x grade FE, clustered SE)

study_label: Wiles et al., Post-experiment knowledge (without AI, 5
notes: No significant difference on any of 5 post-experiment knowledge questions without ChatGPT. No learning retained.

study_label: Wiles et al., Coding task score (with AI access)
notes: Score normalized to data scientist benchmark. Effect during AI use.

study_label: Xu et al., Academic achievement (posttest, ANCOVA a
notes: Both groups used ChatGPT; treatment = metacognitive prompts. F=3.939. 4-week experiment.

study_label: Xu et al., SRL: task strategy
notes: Both groups used ChatGPT; treatment = metacognitive prompts. d from t=2.833.
quality_flags: not RCT; no SE/CI; non-standard outcome

study_label: Chung et al., Standardized in-person final Python cert
outcome: Standardized in-person final Python certification exam (no AI/devices allowed)
treatment: Personalized problem sequencing (POMDP-based RL adaptive algorithm) + GenAI chatbot tutor
control: Fixed problem sequence (easy-to-hard) + same GenAI chatbot tutor
notes: Active control: both arms have same GenAI chatbot; randomization is over adaptive (POMDP+RL) vs. fixed problem sequencing, not AI access. 5-month course in partnership with Taipei City Government. Spec (1): no controls, N=770.

study_label: Chung et al., Standardized in-person final Python cert
outcome: Standardized in-person final Python certification exam (no AI/devices allowed)
treatment: Personalized problem sequencing (POMDP-based RL adaptive algorithm) + GenAI chatbot tutor
control: Fixed problem sequence (easy-to-hard) + same GenAI chatbot tutor
notes: Active control: both arms have same GenAI chatbot; randomization is over adaptive (POMDP+RL) vs. fixed problem sequencing, not AI access. 5-month course in partnership with Taipei City Government. Spec (2): baseline controls + school FE + grade FE, N=716.

study_label: Liu et al., Exp 1 (Math)
treatment: GPT-5 sidebar access during learning phase
notes: Pre-exclusion N=354. Test = 3 fraction problems with AI removed. Skip rate d=+0.25.

study_label: Liu et al., Exp 2 (Math)
outcome: Unassisted solve rate, 3 fraction problems (Exp 2 replication)
treatment: GPT-5 sidebar access during learning phase
control: No AI, with reference sidebar (to match interface)
notes: Pre-exclusion N=667. Exp 2 adds pretest-based exclusions and matched-interface control. Skip rate not significant.

study_label: Liu et al., Exp 3 (Reading)
outcome: Unassisted solve rate, 3 SAT reading-comprehension problems (Exp 3)
treatment: GPT-5 sidebar access during learning phase
notes: Pre-exclusion N=201. Replication in reading-comprehension domain. Skip rate d=+0.42.

study_label: Shen & Tamkin
outcome: Comprehension / code-reading / debugging quiz, no AI
treatment: GPT-4o coding assistant during Trio task
notes: 35-min coding task with AI; 6-question quiz w/o AI. No significant productivity gain. SE back-calculated from d and N.
quality_flags: SE back-calculated from N and Cohen's d
